name: master hollandaise
instructions:
TWO VARIANTS:

VARIANT A — CLASSIC HOLLANDAISE: egg yolk, lemon, ghee/butter, splash of heavy cream. Standard emulsified-butter sauce; pair with asparagus benedict or vegetables.

VARIANT B — TOMATO HOLLANDAISE: classic hollandaise with tomato paste whisked in. Adds depth, umami, and color without breaking the emulsion (paste, not fresh tomato — fresh would add water and split the sauce).
notes:
BASE BUILD-OUT IS IDENTICAL: egg yolk + lemon + ghee/butter as the emulsified base; heavy cream as a stabilizing/richening agent.

TOMATO VARIANT USES PASTE, NOT FRESH OR CANNED: the variant only works with concentrated tomato paste. Fresh and canned tomatoes carry too much water and will break the emulsion. The paste also brings concentrated umami and color in a small volume, which is what you want from this finish.

GHEE OR BUTTER — BOTH WORK: ghee is slightly more forgiving (less likely to break from milk-solid issues) but butter carries more of the classic dairy character. Use what's open.
